raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased round-trip flight tickets with my Barclays credit card through a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Upon returning home, I was shocked when I found an exorbitant charge on my credit card statement. XXXX XXXX claimed they had a reservation that was never finalized, supposedly for the same tickets. If I got the tickets on XX/XX/XXXX, why was I supposed to make a reservation for the same destination? It does not make sense. 

In XXXX, my Barclays issued a refund for the amount, but weeks later, they reversed it, siding with the airline 's claim. Interestingly, the report they provided for Barclays listed my husband as the credit cardholder, although he has no account with Barclays credit card company and is not authorized to use my card.

This situation feels fraudulent ; someone has misused my information, and I would like assistance to resolve this injustice.
